title: Ice Age Dice Show Native Americans Played Games Long Ago
summary: A long time ago, during the Ice Age, Native Americans made dice to play games of chance. These dice were simple with only two sides, not six like today's dice. They were used for fun and to make decisions in games. A researcher named Robert Madden studied old dice found by archaeologists. He found that Native Americans have been using dice for over 12,000 years, which is much earlier than people in other parts of the world. Madden looked at many dice from different tribes and places. He made rules to tell if an old object is a dice. The dice had to be two-sided, easy to tell apart, a certain shape, and small enough to hold. This discovery shows that Native Americans understood how to use random outcomes in games long before others.

a very cold time long ago when ice covered much of the Earth
the first people who lived in America
games where the result is random, like rolling dice

results that happen by chance, not by planning

background_read: This article talks about how Native Americans made and used dice a very long time ago, during the Ice Age. Dice are small objects used in games where the result is random, like rolling to see who wins. Archaeologists, who are like detectives for old things, found these dice in places like Wyoming. A researcher named Robert Madden studied them and found out that Native Americans were playing games with dice over 12,000 years ago, which is much earlier than people in other parts of the world. This shows that they understood how to use chance in fun activities.
Article_Structure: WHO: Robert Madden, a researcher, and Native Americans from long ago.
WHAT: Dice with two sides used for games of chance.
WHERE: Found in places like Wyoming, Colorado, and New Mexico in America.
WHY: To show that Native Americans understood random outcomes in games thousands of years earlier than thought.

perspectives.description: Shows that Native Americans had advanced gaming practices long before other cultures.
